subject: Public Health
title: Ebola in West Africa
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Ebola in West Africa.

hook.text: In 2014, Ebola exploded out of Guinea and overwhelmed three West African nations.

buildup.text: Ebola kills up to 90% of those infected—patients bleed internally as the virus destroys blood vessels.

discovery.text: Weak health systems and distrust of authorities let the outbreak grow unchecked for months.

twist.text: Community engagement—listening to local leaders—proved more effective than military-style quarantines.

climax.text: Over 11,000 died before the outbreak ended. A vaccine was finally approved in 2019.

punchline.text: Trust saved more lives than technology.

quiz.question: What proved most effective in controlling the 2014 Ebola outbreak?
options: Community engagement and listening to local leaders
